tldr: Note: Ably is pub/sub messaging and Dyte is video/audio SDK — this is not a like-for-like swap. Only migrate if your real Ably usage is call/room coordination.
why_switch: Dyte is a fit if your underlying use case is real-time audio/video calls
Freemium entry tier lowers cost floor versus Ably's $29/mo
Strong SDKs and prebuilt UI components
Single vendor for video if you can drop generic pub/sub
what_to_export: Channel history retention settings (and any history you want to preserve)
List of clients using the Ably SDK
export_method: Use Ably's official REST API and dashboard to inventory channels, integration rules and history. Pull persisted history via Ably's history API per their official docs if retention matters. Capture dashboard config (integrations, namespaces) manually. Backend code (token mint, producers/consumers) is canonical and should drive the rebuild.
import_method: Dyte has no general pub/sub primitive — only proceed if your traffic is call-oriented. Sign up for Dyte, generate organization API credentials, and implement server-side meeting creation and participant authentication against Dyte's REST API per their official docs. Swap clients to Dyte's per-platform SDK. For any non-call pub/sub workloads, choose a different target (this guide does not cover that).

concept_mapping.note: Different abstraction; only maps if the channel coordinates a call
Both backend-minted, but claim shapes are vendor-specific
Dyte exposes participants on a meeting; not a global presence service
Limited to a meeting scope; not a generic pub/sub replacement
Dyte is not a message store; persist history yourself if required

effort: 2-6 weeks (longer if Ably usage extends beyond calls)
gotchas: Different product categories — confirm fit before committing
No replacement for persisted history, large-scale presence, MQTT/SSE protocols on Dyte
Token formats and capability models do not translate
Delivery-guarantee semantics differ; load test before cutover
Pricing dimensions differ (messages/peers vs. participant minutes) — verify on Dyte's official pricing page
Run vendors in parallel during cutover and watch for missing events

email.subject: Considering moving Ably real-time workloads to Dyte
email.body:
Hi team,

Flagging a possible migration from Ably to Dyte. Caveat upfront: Ably is pub/sub and Dyte is video/audio, so this only fits if our actual Ably workload is call/room coordination. Other pub/sub use cases would need a separate target.

If fit holds, scope is rebuilding server-side meeting and token issuance on Dyte, swapping clients, and reconfiguring webhooks. Estimating 2-6 weeks depending on how much of our surface is call-shaped. I'll confirm features and pricing from official docs before locking the plan.

Happy to talk through use-case fit first.

Thanks
